steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The word 'However' marks a pivot: the bird's specialization first appears beneficial (aids survival in its habitat) and then becomes a liability (makes it vulnerable to environmental change). The main idea must capture both sides of this trade-off.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The Kaua'i 'amakihi is a species of bird that is related to many other Hawaiian birds but does not share a habitat with any of them.: contradicts the passage, which says nothing about sharing habitat. ✗
  • Choice B — The Kaua'i 'amakihi is an example of a highly specialized bird species found only on the Hawaiian archipelago and is related to several other highly specialized bird species found there.: ignores the vulnerability side of the trade-off entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — The Kaua'i 'amakihi is an example of a species unique to the Hawaiian archipelago that is highly specialized and therefore particularly susceptible to habitat disturbances.: 'therefore' makes the causal link explicit — vulnerability flows directly from specialization. Captures both sides. ✓
  • Choice D — Hawaiian birds display a unique range of physical and behavioral characteristics and as a result can only live in habitats unique to the Hawaiian archipelago.: overgeneralizes from the Kaua'i 'amakihi to Hawaiian birds broadly and omits the vulnerability point. ✗
That is the correct answer.
